item.title: 1979 Is the Year That Explains Donald Trump
item.description: And pretty much all of the 1980s do too.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lemire

item.title: Pete Hegseth Is Trying to Resegregate the Military
item.description: “Color-blind and merit-based” now seems to be anything but.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: Only Losers Play the Madman
item.description: Does Trump seem crazy? Sure. Credible, not so much.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: The Artemis Astronauts Are Studs
item.description: Strength, courage, expertise, wonder: NASA’s moon-mission crew has reset the bar for greatness.
item.dc:creator: Sally Jenkins

item.title: Maybe You’ll Never Really Know Who You’re Marrying
item.description: The fear of commitment transformsThe Dramafrom a romance into a horror story.
item.dc:creator: Shirley Li

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Trying to Erase Its Own History
item.description: If a new legal opinion stands, Donald Trump will be on track to become one of the most poorly documented presidents ever.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The Literary Job AI Can’t Replace
item.description: Ghostwriting is good, actually—when it’s done by humans.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Ackermann

item.title: Trump Made a Deal That Gives Him Nothing He Wanted
item.description: U.S. declarations of victory ring hollow.
item.dc:creator: Nancy A. Youssef

item.title: What It Means to Be American
item.description: Fareed Zakaria and David Frum on whether they regret becoming American citizens. Plus: how 18 years of economic turmoil ushered in a new populist era, and a discussion ofInvisible Citiesby Italo Calvino.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: America Looks Like a Paper Tiger
item.description: The U.S. showed great tactical capabilities in the Iran war, but Iran emerged the winner at a strategic level.
item.dc:creator: Brynn Tannehill

item.title: The Forgotten War That Iran Already Won
item.description: Tehran fought for decades to prevent Israeli-Palestinian peace.
item.dc:creator: Nadav Eyal

item.title: A New Geopolitical Reality Is Here
item.description: America’s adversaries are uniting as its own coalition falls apart.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Wright

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Stone Cold Saudi Arabia
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: ScrubsHas a Sneakily Radical Vision of Male Friendship
item.description: The return of TV’s best bromance comes at an uncertain time for relationships between men.
item.dc:creator: Julie Beck

item.title: The NBA Isn’t the Same for Everyone
item.description: Jaden Ivey’s split with the Chicago Bulls was a brutal reminder of how professional sports often work.
item.dc:creator: Jemele Hill

item.title: Who Is Black Comedy For?
item.description: A new book is nostalgic for the ’90s. But the era of crossover success was not necessarily the pinnacle of Black comedic achievement.
item.dc:creator: K. Austin Collins

item.title: An Incredibly Weird Time to Be Alive
item.description: The world witnessed the best and worst of humanity in a single week.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Warzel

item.title: J. D. Vance Is Definitely Against Foreign Election Interference
item.description: The vice president accused the European Union of meddling—as he stumped for Viktor Orbán.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: What the Astronauts See That Trump Cannot
item.description: Pictures of the Earth from the Artemis II mission offer a sense that humans are united. If only a bellicose president could feel the same.
item.dc:creator: Gal Beckerman

item.title: The Most Beautiful Moment of the Artemis II Mission
item.dc:creator: Ross Andersen
